22: // в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
23: // 1. TYPES
24: 
25: // в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
26: /**
27:  * Normalized definition for a single MCP tool and its JSON schema.
28:  */
29: export interface ToolDefinition {
30:   name: string;
31:   description: string;
32:   inputSchema: Record<string, unknown>;
33:   outputSchema?: Record<string, unknown>;
34: }
35: 
36: export type ToolOwnershipStability = 'stable' | 'deprecated';
37: 
38: export interface ToolOwnershipEntry {
39:   name: string;
40:   owner: string;
41:   stability: ToolOwnershipStability;
42:   level: string;
43:   category: string;
44: }
45: 
46: export interface ToolOwnershipMap {
47:   schemaVersion: 1;
48:   generatedFrom: 'TOOL_DEFINITIONS';
49:   tools: ToolOwnershipEntry[];
50: }
51: 
52: export interface ToolOwnershipDriftReport {
53:   ok: boolean;
54:   missingFromCommitted: string[];
55:   extraInCommitted: string[];
56:   changed: Array<{
57:     name: string;
58:     field: keyof Omit<ToolOwnershipEntry, 'name'>;
59:     expected: string;
60:     actual: string;
61:   }>;
62:   expectedMap: ToolOwnershipMap;
63: }
64: 
65: const TOOL_OWNER_BY_CATEGORY: Record<string, string> = Object.freeze({
66:   Orchestration: 'memory-orchestration',
67:   Core: 'memory-core',
68:   Discovery: 'memory-discovery',
69:   Mutation: 'memory-mutation',
70:   Lifecycle: 'memory-lifecycle',
71:   Analysis: 'memory-analysis',
72:   Maintenance: 'memory-maintenance',
73: });
74: 
75: function readToolContract(definition: ToolDefinition): { level: string; category: string } {
76:   const match = /^\[([^:\]]+):([^\]]+)\]/.exec(definition.description);
77:   if (!match) {
78:     return { level: 'unclassified', category: 'unclassified' };
79:   }
80:   return {
81:     level: match[1] ?? 'unclassified',
82:     category: match[2] ?? 'unclassified',
83:   };
84: }
85: 
86: function deriveToolOwnershipEntry(definition: ToolDefinition): ToolOwnershipEntry {
87:   const contract = readToolContract(definition);
88:   return {
89:     name: definition.name,
90:     owner: TOOL_OWNER_BY_CATEGORY[contract.category] ?? 'memory-unclassified',
91:     stability: /^\[deprecated\]/i.test(definition.description) ? 'deprecated' : 'stable',
92:     level: contract.level,
93:     category: contract.category,
94:   };
95: }
96: 
97: function normalizeToolOwnershipMap(map: ToolOwnershipMap): ToolOwnershipMap {
98:   return {
99:     schemaVersion: 1,
100:     generatedFrom: 'TOOL_DEFINITIONS',
101:     tools: [...map.tools].sort((a, b) => a.name.localeCompare(b.name)),
102:   };
103: }
104: 
105: function isToolOwnershipEntry(value: unknown): value is ToolOwnershipEntry {
106:   if (!value || typeof value !== 'object' || Array.isArray(value)) return false;
107:   const record = value as Record<string, unknown>;
108:   return (
109:     typeof record.name === 'string' &&
110:     typeof record.owner === 'string' &&
111:     (record.stability === 'stable' || record.stability === 'deprecated') &&
112:     typeof record.level === 'string' &&
113:     typeof record.category === 'string'
114:   );
115: }
116: 
117: function parseToolOwnershipMap(value: unknown): ToolOwnershipMap | null {
118:   let parsed: unknown;
119:   try {
120:     parsed = typeof value === 'string' ? JSON.parse(value) as unknown : value;
121:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
122:     return null;
123:   }
124:   if (!parsed || typeof parsed !== 'object' || Array.isArray(parsed)) return null;
125:   const record = parsed as Record<string, unknown>;
126:   if (record.schemaVersion !== 1 || record.generatedFrom !== 'TOOL_DEFINITIONS') return null;
127:   if (!Array.isArray(record.tools) || !record.tools.every(isToolOwnershipEntry)) return null;
128:   return normalizeToolOwnershipMap({
129:     schemaVersion: 1,
130:     generatedFrom: 'TOOL_DEFINITIONS',
131:     tools: record.tools,
132:   });
133: }
134: 
135: /** Derive the deterministic tool ownership map from live tool definitions. */
136: export function deriveToolOwnershipMap(
137:   toolDefinitions: readonly ToolDefinition[] = TOOL_DEFINITIONS,
138: ): ToolOwnershipMap {
139:   return normalizeToolOwnershipMap({
140:     schemaVersion: 1,
141:     generatedFrom: 'TOOL_DEFINITIONS',
142:     tools: toolDefinitions.map(deriveToolOwnershipEntry),
143:   });
144: }
145: 
146: /** Serialize a derived ownership map for byte-identical generated output. */
147: export function serializeToolOwnershipMap(map: ToolOwnershipMap = deriveToolOwnershipMap()): string {
148:   return `${JSON.stringify(normalizeToolOwnershipMap(map), null, 2)}\n`;
149: }
150: 
151: /** Compare a committed ownership map with a fresh derivation. */
152: export function lintToolOwnershipMapDrift(
153:   committedMap: unknown,
154:   toolDefinitions: readonly ToolDefinition[] = TOOL_DEFINITIONS,
155: ): ToolOwnershipDriftReport {
156:   const expectedMap = deriveToolOwnershipMap(toolDefinitions);
157:   const parsed = parseToolOwnershipMap(committedMap);
158:   if (!parsed) {
159:     return {
160:       ok: false,
161:       missingFromCommitted: expectedMap.tools.map((tool) => tool.name),
162:       extraInCommitted: [],
163:       changed: [],
164:       expectedMap,
165:     };
166:   }
167: 
168:   const expectedByName = new Map(expectedMap.tools.map((tool) => [tool.name, tool]));
169:   const actualByName = new Map(parsed.tools.map((tool) => [tool.name, tool]));
170:   const missingFromCommitted = expectedMap.tools
171:     .filter((tool) => !actualByName.has(tool.name))
172:     .map((tool) => tool.name);
173:   const extraInCommitted = parsed.tools
174:     .filter((tool) => !expectedByName.has(tool.name))
175:     .map((tool) => tool.name);
176:   const changed: ToolOwnershipDriftReport['changed'] = [];
177:   const fields: Array<keyof Omit<ToolOwnershipEntry, 'name'>> = ['owner', 'stability', 'level', 'category'];
178: 
179:   for (const expected of expectedMap.tools) {
180:     const actual = actualByName.get(expected.name);
181:     if (!actual) continue;
182:     for (const field of fields) {
183:       if (expected[field] !== actual[field]) {
184:         changed.push({
185:           name: expected.name,
186:           field,
187:           expected: expected[field],
188:           actual: actual[field],
189:         });
190:       }
191:     }
192:   }
193: 
194:   return {
195:     ok: missingFromCommitted.length === 0 && extraInCommitted.length === 0 && changed.length === 0,
196:     missingFromCommitted,
197:     extraInCommitted,
198:     changed,
199:     expectedMap,
200:   };
201: }
202: 
203: // в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
204: // 2. TOOL DEFINITIONS
205: 
206: // в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

1230:               result.content[0].text = JSON.stringify(envelope, null, 2);
1231:             }
1232:           } catch {
1233:             // Response is not JSON envelope вЂ” skip enrichment injection
1234:           }
1235:         }
1236:       } catch (enrichErr: unknown) {
1237:         // Passive enrichment is strictly non-fatal
1238:         const msg = enrichErr instanceof Error ? enrichErr.message : String(enrichErr);
1239:         console.warn(`[context-server] Passive enrichment failed (non-fatal): ${msg}`);
1240:       }
1241:     }
1242: 
1243:     // Inject auto-surface hints before token-budget enforcement so
1244:     // The final envelope metadata reflects the fully decorated response.
1245:     if (autoSurfacedContext && result && !result.isError) {
1246:       appendAutoSurfaceHints(result, autoSurfacedContext);
1247:     }
1248: 
1249:     // Token Budget Hybrid: Inject tokenBudget into response metadata
1250:     // Enforce per-layer token budgets with actual truncation
1251:     if (result && result.content && result.content[0]?.text) {
1252:       try {
1253:         const envelope = JSON.parse(result.content[0].text) as Record<string, unknown>;
1254:         if (envelope && typeof envelope === 'object' && !Array.isArray(envelope)) {
1255:           const metaValue = envelope.meta;
1256:           const meta = isRecord(metaValue)
1257:             ? metaValue as Record<string, unknown>
1258:             : {};
1259:           const dataValue = envelope.data;
1260:           const data = isRecord(dataValue)
1261:             ? dataValue as Record<string, unknown>
1262:             : null;
1263:           envelope.meta = meta;
1264:           if (sessionPrimeContext && !result.isError) {
1265:             injectSessionPrimeHints(envelope, meta, sessionPrimeContext);
1266:           }
1267:           if (autoSurfacedContext && !result.isError) {
1268:             meta.autoSurfacedContext = autoSurfacedContext;
1269:           }
1270:           if (dispatchGraphContext && !result.isError) {
1271:             meta.graphContext = dispatchGraphContext;
1272:           }
1273:           const budget = getTokenBudget(name);
1274:           meta.tokenBudget = budget;
1275:           syncEnvelopeTokenCount(envelope);
1276: 
1277:           if (typeof meta.tokenCount === 'number' && meta.tokenCount > budget) {
1278:             console.error(`[token-budget] ${name} response (${meta.tokenCount} tokens) exceeds budget (${budget})`);
1279: 
1280:             // First-call session priming rides along in meta and can dwarf a
1281:             // small tool's own payload. Slim it BEFORE touching the tool's
1282:             // results: the tool's answer is the caller's data, the priming
1283:             // payload is recoverable via memory_search/memory_context.
1284:             if (isRecord(meta.sessionPriming)) {
1285:               const priming = meta.sessionPriming as Record<string, unknown>;
1286:               const alreadyTrimmed = priming.trimmed === true;
1287:               if (!alreadyTrimmed) {
1288:                 const constitutionalCount = Array.isArray(priming.constitutional)
1289:                   ? priming.constitutional.length
1290:                   : 0;
1291:                 meta.sessionPriming = {
1292:                   trimmed: true,
1293:                   constitutionalCount,
1294:                   ...(isRecord(priming.primePackage) ? { primePackage: priming.primePackage } : {}),
1295:                 };
1296:                 syncEnvelopeTokenCount(envelope);
1297:                 if (Array.isArray(envelope.hints)) {
1298:                   envelope.hints.push(`Session priming trimmed to fit the ${budget} token budget; full constitutional content remains retrievable via memory_search`);
1299:                 }
1300:                 meta.sessionPrimingTrimmed = true;
1301:               }
1302:             }
1303: 
1304:             // Attempt to truncate results array to fit within budget вЂ” only
1305:             // when still over after the priming slim above, because the loop
1306:             // below pops a result before it re-measures.
1307:             const stillOverBudget = typeof meta.tokenCount === 'number' && meta.tokenCount > budget;
1308:             const innerResults = data?.results;
1309:             if (stillOverBudget && Array.isArray(innerResults) && innerResults.length > 1) {
1310:               const originalCount = innerResults.length;
1311:               // Results are typically sorted by score (highest first)
1312:               // Remove from end (lowest-scored) until within budget
1313:               while (innerResults.length > 1) {
1314:                 innerResults.pop();

1316:                 // (not just results) so trace metadata is included in the budget.
1317:                 syncEnvelopeTokenCount(envelope);
1318:                 if (typeof meta.tokenCount === 'number' && meta.tokenCount <= budget) break;
1319:               }
1320:               if (data && data.count !== undefined) {

1322:               }
1323:               // Recompute constitutionalCount from the results that survived truncation
1324:               // so consumers reading data.constitutionalCount or envelope.summary see
1325:               // counts that agree with data.results.length.
1326:               if (data) {
1327:                 const survivingConstitutionalCount = innerResults.filter(
1328:                   (r: unknown) => r !== null && typeof r === 'object' && (r as Record<string, unknown>).isConstitutional === true
1329:                 ).length;
1330:                 data.constitutionalCount = survivingConstitutionalCount;
1331:                 if (typeof envelope.summary === 'string') {
1332:                   envelope.summary = survivingConstitutionalCount > 0

1334:                     : `Found ${innerResults.length} memories`;
1335:                 }
1336:               }
1337:               if (Array.isArray(envelope.hints)) {
1338:                 envelope.hints.push(`Token budget enforced: truncated ${originalCount} в†’ ${innerResults.length} results to fit ${budget} token budget`);
1339:               }
1340:               meta.tokenBudgetTruncated = true;
1341:               meta.originalResultCount = originalCount;
1342:               meta.returnedResultCount = innerResults.length;
1343:             } else if (stillOverBudget) {

1345:               if (Array.isArray(envelope.hints)) {

2: // MODULE: Paths

4: 
5: import fs from 'fs';
6: import path from 'path';
7: 
8: import { getDbDir } from './config.js';

10: 
11: function findUp(startDir: string, predicate: (dir: string) => boolean): string | undefined {
12:   let dir = startDir;
13:   while (true) {
14:     if (predicate(dir)) return dir;
15:     const parent = path.dirname(dir);
16:     if (parent === dir) return undefined;
17:     dir = parent;
18:   }
19: }
20: 
21: function hasPackageJson(dir: string): boolean {
22:   return fs.existsSync(path.join(dir, 'package.json'));
23: }
24: 
25: function hasWorkspaceDirectories(dir: string): boolean {
26:   return (
27:     fs.existsSync(path.join(dir, 'mcp_server')) &&
28:     fs.existsSync(path.join(dir, 'shared'))
29:   );
30: }
31: 
32: function findNearestSpecKitWorkspaceRoot(startDir: string): string | undefined {
33:   // Walk up to find the workspace root (package.json with "workspaces" field),
34:   // NOT individual @spec-kit/* packages. This ensures path validation uses the
35:   // monorepo root as the boundary, not a child package like shared/.
36:   return findUp(startDir, (dir) => {
37:     const packageJsonPath = path.join(dir, 'package.json');
38:     if (!fs.existsSync(packageJsonPath)) return false;
39:     try {
40:       const parsed = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(packageJsonPath, 'utf-8')) as { workspaces?: unknown };
41:       return Array.isArray(parsed.workspaces);
42:     } catch { return false; }
43:   });
44: }
45: 
46: function isWithinDirectoryTree(candidatePath: string, rootPath: string): boolean {
47:   const relativePath = path.relative(rootPath, candidatePath);
48:   return relativePath === '' || (!relativePath.startsWith('..') && !path.isAbsolute(relativePath));
49: }
50: 
51: function resolveImportMetaRelativePackageRoot(): string {
52:   return path.resolve(import.meta.dirname, '..');
53: }
54: 
55: function fallbackResolvedPath(label: 'package root' | 'database dir'): string {
56:   return label === 'package root'
57:     ? resolveImportMetaRelativePackageRoot()
58:     : path.join(resolveImportMetaRelativePackageRoot(), 'mcp_server', 'database');
59: }
60: 
61: function validateResolvedPath(label: 'package root' | 'database dir', resolvedPath: string): string {
62:   const workspaceRoot = findNearestSpecKitWorkspaceRoot(import.meta.dirname);
63:   if (!workspaceRoot || isWithinDirectoryTree(resolvedPath, workspaceRoot)) {
64:     return resolvedPath;
65:   }
66: 
67:   console.warn(`[shared/paths] ${label} resolved outside @spec-kit workspace root (${workspaceRoot}); falling back to import.meta.dirname-relative resolution`);
68:   return fallbackResolvedPath(label);
69: }
70: 
71: export function resolvePackageRoot(): string {
72:   const fromWorkspaceDirs = findUp(import.meta.dirname, hasWorkspaceDirectories);
73:   if (fromWorkspaceDirs) {
74:     return validateResolvedPath('package root', fromWorkspaceDirs);
75:   }
76: 
77:   const fromWorkspaceDirsCwd = findUp(process.cwd(), hasWorkspaceDirectories);
78:   if (fromWorkspaceDirsCwd) {
79:     return validateResolvedPath('package root', fromWorkspaceDirsCwd);
80:   }
81: 
82:   const fromPackageJson = findUp(import.meta.dirname, hasPackageJson);
83:   if (fromPackageJson) {
84:     return validateResolvedPath('package root', fromPackageJson);
85:   }
86: 
87:   const fromCwd = findUp(process.cwd(), hasPackageJson);
88:   return validateResolvedPath('package root', fromCwd || resolveImportMetaRelativePackageRoot());
89: }
90: 
91: export function resolveDatabaseDir(): string {
92:   const configuredDir = getDbDir();
93:   if (configuredDir) {
94:     return validateResolvedPath('database dir', path.resolve(process.cwd(), configuredDir));
95:   }
96:   return validateResolvedPath('database dir', path.join(resolvePackageRoot(), 'mcp_server', 'database'));
97: }
98: 
99: function resolveDerivedDbPath(databaseDir = resolveDatabaseDir()): string {
100:   return getStartupEmbeddingProfile().getDatabasePath(databaseDir);
101: }
102: 
103: /** Defines database path. */
104: export const DB_PATH: string = (() => {
105:   const configuredPath = process.env.MEMORY_DB_PATH?.trim();
106:   if (configuredPath) {
107:     const resolvedPath = path.resolve(process.cwd(), configuredPath);
108:     const validatedDir = validateResolvedPath('database dir', path.dirname(resolvedPath));
109:     return path.join(validatedDir, path.basename(resolvedPath));
110:   }
111: 
112:   return resolveDerivedDbPath();
113: })();

27:   ProviderInfo,
28:   ApiKeyValidationResult,
29:   TaskPrefixMap,
30:   TaskType,
31:   EmbeddingProfileData,
32: } from './types.js';
33: 
34: export interface WeightedDocumentSections {
35:   title?: string | null;
36:   decisions?: string[];
37:   outcomes?: string[];
38:   general?: string | null;
39: }
40: 
41: // ---------------------------------------------------------------
42: // EMBEDDING CIRCUIT BREAKER
43: // ---------------------------------------------------------------
44: // Mirrors the embedder-sidecar circuit breaker pattern.
45: // After EMBEDDING_CB_THRESHOLD consecutive failures the circuit opens
46: // and embedding calls return null immediately for EMBEDDING_CB_COOLDOWN_MS,
47: // letting the search pipeline fall back to keyword/BM25 channels.
48: 
49: /** Master kill-switch: set SPECKIT_EMBEDDING_CIRCUIT_BREAKER=false to disable. */
50: function isEmbeddingCircuitBreakerEnabled(): boolean {
51:   const v = process.env.SPECKIT_EMBEDDING_CIRCUIT_BREAKER?.toLowerCase().trim();
52:   if (v === 'false' || v === '0') return false;
53:   return true; // default ON
54: }
55: 
56: const EMBEDDING_CB_THRESHOLD: number =
57:   Math.max(1, parseInt(process.env.SPECKIT_EMBEDDING_CB_THRESHOLD || '', 10) || 3);
58: 
59: const EMBEDDING_CB_COOLDOWN_MS: number =
60:   Math.max(1000, parseInt(process.env.SPECKIT_EMBEDDING_CB_COOLDOWN_MS || '', 10) || 60_000);
61: 
62: interface EmbeddingCircuitState {
63:   failures: number;
64:   openedAt: number | null;
65: }
66: 
67: const embeddingCircuit: EmbeddingCircuitState = { failures: 0, openedAt: null };
68: 
69: function isEmbeddingCircuitOpen(): boolean {
70:   if (!isEmbeddingCircuitBreakerEnabled()) return false;
71:   if (embeddingCircuit.openedAt === null) return false;
72:   if (Date.now() - embeddingCircuit.openedAt >= EMBEDDING_CB_COOLDOWN_MS) {
73:     // Cooldown elapsed вЂ” half-open: allow one attempt
74:     console.warn(
75:       `[embeddings] Circuit breaker HALF-OPEN вЂ” cooldown elapsed (${EMBEDDING_CB_COOLDOWN_MS}ms). Allowing probe request.`
76:     );
77:     embeddingCircuit.openedAt = null;
78:     embeddingCircuit.failures = 0;
79:     return false;
80:   }
81:   return true;
82: }
83: 
84: function recordEmbeddingSuccess(): void {
85:   if (embeddingCircuit.failures > 0 || embeddingCircuit.openedAt !== null) {
86:     console.warn('[embeddings] Circuit breaker CLOSED вЂ” embedding call succeeded.');
87:   }
88:   embeddingCircuit.failures = 0;
89:   embeddingCircuit.openedAt = null;
90: }
91: 
92: function recordEmbeddingFailure(): void {
93:   if (!isEmbeddingCircuitBreakerEnabled()) return;
94:   embeddingCircuit.failures++;
95:   if (embeddingCircuit.failures >= EMBEDDING_CB_THRESHOLD && embeddingCircuit.openedAt === null) {
96:     embeddingCircuit.openedAt = Date.now();
97:     console.warn(
98:       `[embeddings] Circuit breaker OPEN after ${embeddingCircuit.failures} consecutive failures. ` +
99:       `Cooldown: ${EMBEDDING_CB_COOLDOWN_MS}ms. Embedding calls will return null (keyword/BM25 fallback).`
100:     );
101:   }
102: }
103: 
104: // ---------------------------------------------------------------
105: // 1. EMBEDDING CACHE
106: // ---------------------------------------------------------------
107: 
108: const EMBEDDING_CACHE_MAX_SIZE: number = 1000;
109: const embeddingCache: Map<string, Float32Array> = new Map();
110: 
111: // ---------------------------------------------------------------
112: // RATE LIMITING CONFIGURATION
113: // ---------------------------------------------------------------
114: 
115: /**
116:  * Delay between batch embedding requests (ms).
117:  * Prevents overwhelming external embedding providers (Voyage, OpenAI).
118:  * Configurable via EMBEDDING_BATCH_DELAY_MS environment variable.
119:  * Default: 100ms (allows ~10 requests/second, well under typical rate limits)
120:  */
121: const BATCH_DELAY_MS: number = parseInt(process.env.EMBEDDING_BATCH_DELAY_MS || '', 10) || 100;
122: 
123: /**
124:  * Sleep helper for rate limiting
125:  */
126: function sleep(ms: number): Promise<void> {
127:   return new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, ms));
128: }
129: 
130: interface ErrorWithStatus {
131:   status?: number;
132: }
133: 
134: function getErrorMessage(error: unknown): string {
135:   return error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
136: }
137: 
138: function getErrorStatus(error: unknown): number | undefined {
139:   if (typeof error !== 'object' || error === null || !('status' in error)) {
140:     return undefined;
141:   }
142: 
143:   const status = Reflect.get(error, 'status');
144:   return typeof status === 'number' ? status : undefined;
145: }
146: 
147: function normalizeWeightedSectionText(text: string | null | undefined): string {
148:   if (typeof text !== 'string') {
149:     return '';
150:   }
151: 
152:   return text
153:     .replace(/\r\n/g, '\n')
154:     .replace(/[ \t]+/g, ' ')
155:     .replace(/\n{3,}/g, '\n\n')
156:     .trim();
157: }
158: 
159: function trimToWordBoundary(text: string, maxLength: number): string {
160:   if (text.length <= maxLength) {
161:     return text;
162:   }
163: 
164:   if (maxLength <= MIN_SECTION_LENGTH) {
165:     return '';
166:   }
167: 
168:   const hardTrimmed = text.slice(0, maxLength).trimEnd();
169:   const lastWhitespace = Math.max(
170:     hardTrimmed.lastIndexOf(' '),
171:     hardTrimmed.lastIndexOf('\n')
172:   );
173: 
174:   if (lastWhitespace >= Math.floor(maxLength * 0.6)) {
175:     return hardTrimmed.slice(0, lastWhitespace).trimEnd();
176:   }
177: 
178:   return hardTrimmed;
179: }
180: 
181: function buildWeightedSectionBlock(entries: string[], multiplier: number): string {
182:   if (entries.length === 0 || multiplier <= 0) {
183:     return '';
184:   }
185: 
186:   const weightedEntries: string[] = [];
187:   for (const entry of entries) {
188:     for (let index = 0; index < multiplier; index++) {
189:       weightedEntries.push(entry);
190:     }
191:   }
192: 
193:   return weightedEntries.join('\n\n');
194: }
195: 
196: function trimWeightedBlock(block: string, overflow: number): string {
197:   if (!block || overflow <= 0) {
198:     return block;
199:   }
200: 
201:   const nextLength = Math.max(0, block.length - overflow);
202:   return trimToWordBoundary(block, nextLength);
203: }
204: 
205: function joinWeightedBlocks(blocks: string[]): string {
206:   return blocks.filter(Boolean).join('\n\n').trim();
207: }
208: 
209: function prepareWeightedEntries(entries: string[] | null | undefined): string[] {
210:   if (!Array.isArray(entries)) {
211:     return [];
212:   }
213: 
214:   return entries
215:     .map((entry) => normalizeWeightedSectionText(entry))
216:     .filter(Boolean);
217: }
218: 
219: function buildGeneralWeightedBlock(sections: WeightedDocumentSections): string {
220:   const general = normalizeWeightedSectionText(sections.general);
221:   return general;
222: }
223: 
224: function buildDecisionWeightedBlock(sections: WeightedDocumentSections): string {
225:   return buildWeightedSectionBlock(prepareWeightedEntries(sections.decisions), 3);
226: }
227: 
228: function buildOutcomeWeightedBlock(sections: WeightedDocumentSections): string {
229:   return buildWeightedSectionBlock(prepareWeightedEntries(sections.outcomes), 2);
230: }
231: 
232: function buildTitleWeightedBlock(sections: WeightedDocumentSections): string {
233:   return normalizeWeightedSectionText(sections.title);
234: }
235: 
236: function truncateWeightedBlocksToBudget(
237:   blocks: { title: string; decisions: string; outcomes: string; general: string },
238:   maxLength: number,
239: ): { title: string; decisions: string; outcomes: string; general: string } {
240:   let nextBlocks = { ...blocks };
241:   let combined = joinWeightedBlocks([
242:     nextBlocks.title,
243:     nextBlocks.decisions,
244:     nextBlocks.outcomes,
245:     nextBlocks.general,
246:   ]);
247: 
248:   if (combined.length <= maxLength) {
249:     return nextBlocks;
250:   }
251: 
252:   let overflow = combined.length - maxLength;
253:   nextBlocks.general = trimWeightedBlock(nextBlocks.general, overflow);
254:   combined = joinWeightedBlocks([nextBlocks.title, nextBlocks.decisions, nextBlocks.outcomes, nextBlocks.general]);
255: 
256:   if (combined.length <= maxLength) {
257:     return nextBlocks;
258:   }
259: 
260:   overflow = combined.length - maxLength;
261:   nextBlocks.outcomes = trimWeightedBlock(nextBlocks.outcomes, overflow);
262:   combined = joinWeightedBlocks([nextBlocks.title, nextBlocks.decisions, nextBlocks.outcomes, nextBlocks.general]);
263: 
264:   if (combined.length <= maxLength) {
265:     return nextBlocks;
266:   }
267: 
268:   overflow = combined.length - maxLength;
269:   nextBlocks.decisions = trimWeightedBlock(nextBlocks.decisions, overflow);
270:   combined = joinWeightedBlocks([nextBlocks.title, nextBlocks.decisions, nextBlocks.outcomes, nextBlocks.general]);
271: 
272:   if (combined.length <= maxLength) {
273:     return nextBlocks;
274:   }
275: 
276:   overflow = combined.length - maxLength;
277:   nextBlocks.title = trimWeightedBlock(nextBlocks.title, overflow);
278:   return nextBlocks;
279: }
280: 
281: function buildWeightedDocumentText(
282:   sections: WeightedDocumentSections,
283:   maxLength: number = MAX_TEXT_LENGTH,
284: ): string {
285:   const safeMaxLength = Number.isFinite(maxLength) && maxLength > 0 ? Math.floor(maxLength) : MAX_TEXT_LENGTH;
286:   const blocks = truncateWeightedBlocksToBudget({
287:     title: buildTitleWeightedBlock(sections),
288:     decisions: buildDecisionWeightedBlock(sections),
289:     outcomes: buildOutcomeWeightedBlock(sections),
290:     general: buildGeneralWeightedBlock(sections),
291:   }, safeMaxLength);
292: 
293:   return joinWeightedBlocks([blocks.title, blocks.decisions, blocks.outcomes, blocks.general]);
294: }
295: 
296: /**
297:  * Generate SHA256 hash key for cache lookup.
298:  * Uses 32 hex chars (128 bits) for cache keys.
299:  * Keys are scoped by provider name to prevent cross-provider cache hits
300:  * (different providers produce different-dimension embeddings).
301:  *
302:  * Collision Analysis:
303:  * - 32 hex chars = 128 bits = 2^128 possible values
304:  * - With 1000 cache entries, birthday paradox collision probability ~ 0
305:  * - Even with 10^18 entries, collision probability < 10^-20
306:  */
307: function getCacheKey(text: string): string {
308:   const providerName = providerInstance ? providerInstance.getMetadata().provider : 'unknown';
309:   return crypto.createHash('sha256').update(`${providerName}:${text}`).digest('hex').substring(0, 32);
310: }
311: 
312: /** Get cached embedding or null */
313: function getCachedEmbedding(text: string): Float32Array | null {
314:   const key = getCacheKey(text);
315:   const cached = embeddingCache.get(key);
316:   if (cached) {
317:     embeddingCache.delete(key);
318:     embeddingCache.set(key, cached);
319:     return cached;
320:   }
321:   return null;
322: }
323: 
324: /** Store embedding in cache with LRU eviction */
325: function cacheEmbedding(text: string, embedding: Float32Array): void {
326:   const key = getCacheKey(text);
327:   if (embeddingCache.size >= EMBEDDING_CACHE_MAX_SIZE) {
328:     const firstKey = embeddingCache.keys().next().value;
329:     if (firstKey !== undefined) {
330:       embeddingCache.delete(firstKey);
331:     }
332:   }
333:   embeddingCache.set(key, embedding);
334: }
335: 
336: function clearEmbeddingCache(): void {
337:   embeddingCache.clear();
338: }
339: 
340: function getEmbeddingCacheStats(): EmbeddingCacheStats {
341:   return {
342:     size: embeddingCache.size,
343:     maxSize: EMBEDDING_CACHE_MAX_SIZE,
344:   };
345: }
346:

348: // 2. LAZY SINGLETON PROVIDER INSTANCE
349: // ---------------------------------------------------------------

352:  * LAZY SINGLETON PATTERN

354:  * The embedding provider is initialized lazily on first use to reduce
355:  * MCP startup time from 2-3s to <500ms.
356:  *
357:  * Initialization Flow:
358:  * 1. On first embedding request, get_provider() creates the instance
359:  * 2. Provider is created without warmup (warmup: false)
360:  * 3. First actual embedding call triggers model loading

1: // в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
2: // MODULE: Chunking
3: // в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
4: 
5: // Feature catalog: Anchor-aware chunk thinning
6: 
7: import type { PriorityPatterns, PriorityBuckets } from './types.js';
8: 
9: // ---------------------------------------------------------------
10: // 1. CONFIGURATION
11: //
12: // These constants are the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for text length limits.
13: // Other modules (embeddings.ts, hf-local.ts) import from here.
14: // ---------------------------------------------------------------
15: 
16: /**
17:  * Maximum text length for embedding generation.
18:  * This value is used across all embedding providers.
19:  * Based on the local embedding profile window used by current defaults.
20:  */
21: export const MAX_TEXT_LENGTH: number = 8000;
22: /** Defines reserved overview. */
23: export const RESERVED_OVERVIEW: number = 500;
24: /** Defines reserved outcome. */
25: export const RESERVED_OUTCOME: number = 300;
26: /** Defines min section length. */
27: export const MIN_SECTION_LENGTH: number = 20;
28: 
29: const PRIORITY_PATTERNS: PriorityPatterns = {
30:   high: /overview|summary|decision|chose|key outcome|conclusion|result|important|critical|must|required/i,
31:   medium: /implementation|file|change|technical|approach|solution|method|function|class|component/i,
32: };
33: 
34: /**
35:  * Truncate `text` to at most `maxLength` UTF-16 code units without splitting a
36:  * surrogate pair, preferring the nearest preceding whitespace so words are not
37:  * cut mid-token. Falls back to a code-point-aligned hard cut when the truncated
38:  * region contains no usable whitespace. The returned length never exceeds
39:  * `maxLength` вЂ” downstream embedders rely on that hard cap.
40:  */
41: function truncateToLength(text: string, maxLength: number): string {
42:   if (maxLength <= 0) return '';
43:   if (text.length <= maxLength) return text;
44: 
45:   // Walk code points so a multi-unit character is never partially included.
46:   let cutIndex = 0;
47:   let unitCount = 0;
48:   for (const ch of text) {
49:     if (unitCount + ch.length > maxLength) break;
50:     unitCount += ch.length;
51:     cutIndex += ch.length;
52:   }
53: 
54:   let sliced = text.slice(0, cutIndex);
55:   const lastWhitespace = sliced.search(/\s\S*$/);
56:   if (lastWhitespace > 0) {
57:     sliced = sliced.slice(0, lastWhitespace);
58:   }
59:   return sliced;
60: }
61: 
62: // ---------------------------------------------------------------
63: // 2. SEMANTIC CHUNKING
64: // ---------------------------------------------------------------
65: 
66: /**
67:  * Intelligently chunk text to fit within max_length while preserving important content.
68:  * Priority: 1) First RESERVED_OVERVIEW chars, 2) Last RESERVED_OUTCOME chars,
69:  * 3) Decision sections, 4) Fill remaining with high -> medium -> low priority.
70:  */
71: export function semanticChunk(text: string, maxLength: number = MAX_TEXT_LENGTH): string {
72:   if (text.length <= maxLength) {
73:     return text;
74:   }
75: 
76:   const originalLength = text.length;
77: 
78:   const overview = text.substring(0, RESERVED_OVERVIEW);
79:   const outcome = text.substring(text.length - RESERVED_OUTCOME);
80: 
81:   let remainingBudget = maxLength - overview.length - outcome.length - 10;
82: 
83:   const middleText = text.substring(RESERVED_OVERVIEW, text.length - RESERVED_OUTCOME);
84:   const sections = middleText.split(/\n#{1,3}\s|\n\n/).filter(s => s.trim().length >= MIN_SECTION_LENGTH);
85: 
86:   const priorities: PriorityBuckets = {
87:     critical: [],
88:     high: [],
89:     medium: [],
90:     low: [],
91:   };
92: 
93:   sections.forEach((section: string) => {
94:     const trimmed = section.trim();
95:     if (trimmed.length < MIN_SECTION_LENGTH) return;
96: 
97:     if (/decision|chose|decided|selected|picked|concluded/i.test(trimmed)) {
98:       priorities.critical.push(trimmed);
99:     } else if (PRIORITY_PATTERNS.high.test(trimmed)) {
100:       priorities.high.push(trimmed);
101:     } else if (PRIORITY_PATTERNS.medium.test(trimmed)) {
102:       priorities.medium.push(trimmed);
103:     } else {
104:       priorities.low.push(trimmed);
105:     }
106:   });
107: 
108:   const includedSections: string[] = [];
109: 
110:   for (const section of priorities.critical) {
111:     // Critical sections are highest priority but still bounded by the budget;
112:     // without this guard, many oversized critical sections drive the budget
113:     // deeply negative and force a mid-content hard truncate downstream.
114:     if (remainingBudget <= 0) break;
115:     includedSections.push(section);
116:     remainingBudget -= section.length + 2;
117:   }
118: 
119:   for (const priority of ['high', 'medium', 'low'] as const) {
120:     for (const section of priorities[priority]) {
121:       if (remainingBudget <= 0) break;
122:       if (section.length + 2 <= remainingBudget) {
123:         includedSections.push(section);
124:         remainingBudget -= section.length + 2;
125:       }
126:     }
127:     if (remainingBudget <= 0) break;
128:   }
129: 
130:   let result = overview;
131:   if (includedSections.length > 0) {
132:     result += '\n\n' + includedSections.join('\n\n');
133:   }
134:   result += '\n\n' + outcome;
135: 
136:   if (result.length > maxLength) {
137:     result = truncateToLength(result, maxLength);
138:   }
139: 
140:   console.warn(`[chunking] Semantic chunking: ${originalLength} -> ${result.length} chars (${Math.round((1 - result.length / originalLength) * 100)}% reduced)`);
141: 
142:   return result.trim();
143: }

480: /**
481:  * Generate embedding for text (low-level function).
482:  * First call triggers lazy model initialization.
483:  * Circuit breaker: returns null immediately when the provider is failing.
484:  */
485: async function generateEmbedding(text: string): Promise<Float32Array | null> {
486:   if (!text || typeof text !== 'string') {
487:     console.warn('[embeddings] Empty or invalid text provided');
488:     return null;
489:   }
490: 
491:   const trimmedText = text.trim();
492:   if (trimmedText.length === 0) {
493:     console.warn('[embeddings] Empty text after trim');
494:     return null;
495:   }
496: 
497:   const cached = getCachedEmbedding(trimmedText);
498:   if (cached) {
499:     return cached;

501: 
502:   // Circuit breaker: skip provider call when open
503:   if (isEmbeddingCircuitOpen()) {
504:     return null;
505:   }
506: 
507:   // Track first embedding time for lazy loading diagnostics
508:   const isFirstEmbedding = !firstEmbeddingTime && !isProviderInitialized();
509: 
510:   try {
511:     const provider = await getProvider();
512: 
513:     // Record first embedding timestamp after provider init
514:     if (isFirstEmbedding && !firstEmbeddingTime) {
515:       firstEmbeddingTime = Date.now();
516:     }
517: 
518:     const maxLength = MAX_TEXT_LENGTH;
519:     let inputText = trimmedText;
520:     if (inputText.length > maxLength) {
521:       inputText = semanticChunk(trimmedText, maxLength);
522:     }
523: 
524:     const embedding = await provider.generateEmbedding(inputText);
525: 
526:     if (embedding) {
527:       recordEmbeddingSuccess();
528:       cacheEmbedding(trimmedText, embedding);
529:     }
530: 
531:     return embedding;
532:   } catch (error: unknown) {
533:     recordEmbeddingFailure();
534:     const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
535:     console.warn(`[embeddings] generateEmbedding failed: ${msg}`);
536:     throw error;
537:   }
538: }

540: /** Generate embedding with timeout protection (default: 30s) */
541: async function generateEmbeddingWithTimeout(text: string, timeout: number = 30000): Promise<Float32Array | null> {
542:   const timeoutPromise = new Promise<never>((_, reject) => {
543:     setTimeout(() => reject(new Error('Embedding generation timed out')), timeout);
544:   });

546:   return Promise.race([
547:     generateEmbedding(text),
548:     timeoutPromise,
549:   ]);

552: /**
553:  * Generate embeddings for batch of texts with parallel processing and rate limiting.
554:  *
555:  * Rate limiting prevents overwhelming external embedding providers (Voyage, OpenAI)
556:  * by adding a configurable delay between batch requests.
557:  * On 429 (rate limit) errors, implements exponential backoff before retrying the batch.
558:  */
559: async function generateBatchEmbeddings(
560:   texts: string[],
561:   concurrency: number = 5,

7: import path from 'path';

10: // ---------------------------------------------------------------
11: // 1. PATH VALIDATION
12: // ---------------------------------------------------------------
13: 
14: /**
15:  * Validate file path is within allowed directories (CWE-22: Path Traversal mitigation)
16:  * Uses path.relative() containment check instead of startsWith() to prevent path confusion attacks.
17:  */
18: export function validateFilePath(filePath: string, allowedBasePaths: string[]): string | null {
19:   if (!filePath || typeof filePath !== 'string') {
20:     return null;
21:   }
22: 
23:   if (!Array.isArray(allowedBasePaths) || allowedBasePaths.length === 0) {
24:     console.warn('[utils] validateFilePath called with empty allowedBasePaths');
25:     return null;
26:   }
27: 
28:   try {
29:     // Security: Reject null bytes BEFORE path.resolve() to prevent path truncation (CWE-78)
30:     if (filePath.includes('\0')) {
31:       console.warn(`[utils] Null byte in path blocked: ${filePath}`);
32:       return null;
33:     }
34: 
35:     // Security: Reject explicit traversal segments before normalization.
36:     // Even if resolution lands inside an allowed base, a caller supplying ".."
37:     // is treated as a traversal attempt and blocked.
38:     const normalizedInput = filePath.replace(/\\/g, '/');
39:     if (normalizedInput.split('/').some((segment) => segment === '..')) {
40:       console.warn(`[utils] Path traversal segment blocked: ${filePath}`);
41:       return null;
42:     }
43: 
44:     const resolved = path.resolve(filePath);
45: 
46:     // Compare canonical paths so symlink aliases cannot bypass containment checks.
47:     let realResolved: string;
48:     try {
49:       realResolved = fs.realpathSync(resolved);
50:     } catch (_error: unknown) {
51:       if (_error instanceof Error) {
52:         void _error.message;
53:       }
54:       // New files may not exist yet, so we canonicalize the parent and rebuild the target path.
55:       try {
56:         const parentReal = fs.realpathSync(path.dirname(resolved));
57:         realResolved = path.join(parentReal, path.basename(resolved));
58:       } catch (_error: unknown) {
59:         if (_error instanceof Error) {
60:           void _error.message;
61:         }
62:         // AI-WHY: When neither the target file nor its parent directory exist on disk
63:         // (e.g. a new file in a not-yet-created directory), realpathSync cannot
64:         // canonicalize the path. Falling back to path.resolve() is safe here because
65:         // the subsequent path.relative() containment check still prevents traversal вЂ”
66:         // resolve() normalises away ".." segments, so a malicious path like
67:         // "/allowed/../etc/passwd" becomes "/etc/passwd" which will correctly fail
68:         // the containment test against allowedBasePaths.
69:         realResolved = resolved;
70:       }
71:     }
72: 
73:     // Security: path.relative() containment check (CWE-22)
74:     const isAllowed = allowedBasePaths.some((basePath) => {
75:       try {
76:         const normalizedBase = path.resolve(basePath);
77:         // Canonicalize base paths too, otherwise realResolved and base could use different aliases.
78:         let realBase: string;
79:         try {
80:           realBase = fs.realpathSync(normalizedBase);
81:         } catch (_error: unknown) {
82:           if (_error instanceof Error) {
83:             void _error.message;
84:           }
85:           realBase = normalizedBase;
86:         }
87:         const relative = path.relative(realBase, realResolved);
88:         return relative === '' || (!relative.startsWith('..') && !path.isAbsolute(relative));
89:       } catch (_error: unknown) {
90:         if (_error instanceof Error) {
91:           void _error.message;
92:         }
93:         return false;
94:       }
95:     });
96: 
97:     if (!isAllowed) {
98:       console.warn(`[utils] Path traversal blocked: ${filePath} -> ${realResolved}`);
99:       return null;
100:     }
101: 
102:     return realResolved;
103:   } catch (error: unknown) {
104:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
105:     console.warn(`[utils] Path validation error: ${message}`);
106:     return null;
107:   }
108: }

116:   return str.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, '\\$&');
117: }

2044:     const memoryCountRow = database.prepare('SELECT COUNT(*) as cnt FROM memory_index').get() as { cnt?: number } | undefined;
2045:     const memoryCount = Number(memoryCountRow?.cnt ?? 0);
2046:     let projectionCount = 0;
2047:     let projectionTableExists = true;
2048: 
2049:     try {
2050:       const projectionCountRow = database.prepare('SELECT COUNT(*) as cnt FROM active_memory_projection').get() as { cnt?: number } | undefined;
2051:       projectionCount = Number(projectionCountRow?.cnt ?? 0);
2052:     } catch {

2063:     if (memoryCount === 0 && projectionCount === 0) {

2081:     runCheckpointNeedsRebuildRepair(database, 'boot');

2083:     // P3-04/014: Warm in-memory BM25 only when the selected lexical engine needs it.
2084:     if (bm25Index.shouldWarmInMemoryBm25(database)) {
2085:       try {
2086:         const bm25 = bm25Index.getIndex();
2087:         const count = bm25.rebuildFromDatabase(database);
2088:         console.error(`[context-server] BM25 index rebuilt from database: ${count} documents`);
2089:       } catch (bm25Err: unknown) {
2090:         const message = bm25Err instanceof Error ? bm25Err.message : String(bm25Err);
2091:         console.warn('[context-server] BM25 index rebuild failed:', message);
2092:       }
2093:     }
2094: 
2095:     // Cognitive memory modules
2096:     try {
2097:       workingMemory.init(database);
2098:       attentionDecay.init(database);
2099:       coActivation.init(database);
2100:       console.error('[context-server] Cognitive memory modules initialized');
2101:       console.error(`[context-server] Working memory: ${workingMemory.isEnabled()}, Co-activation: ${coActivation.isEnabled()}`);
2102:     } catch (cognitiveErr: unknown) {
2103:       const message = cognitiveErr instanceof Error ? cognitiveErr.message : String(cognitiveErr);
2104:       console.warn('[context-server] Cognitive modules partially failed:', message);
2105:     }
2106: 
2107:     try {
2108:       initExtractionAdapter(database, registerAfterToolCallback);
2109:       console.error('[context-server] Extraction adapter initialized');
2110:     } catch (extractionErr: unknown) {
2111:       const message = extractionErr instanceof Error ? extractionErr.message : String(extractionErr);
2112:       throw new Error(`[context-server] Extraction adapter startup failed: ${message}`);
2113:     }
